requirements.text: The system shall allow shoppers to navigate from the main storefront into a dedicated gift-card area.
requirements.annotation_notes: The flow starts on the general retail homepage and then shows a dedicated UA Gift Cards page, indicating supported navigation from the storefront into the gift-card experience.
requirements.manual_verification_notes: the navigation is not really fulfilled, since the "Gift Cards" link is only very small in the footer

requirements.text: The system shall provide a gift-card section that supports both gift-card purchasing and gift-card balance inquiry.
requirements.annotation_notes: The gift-card landing page presents purchasable gift-card options and a separate balance-check form, showing both capabilities in one area.

requirements.text: The system shall distinguish between physical gift cards and eGift cards as separate gift-card purchase modalities.
requirements.annotation_notes: The landing page shows UA Gift Cards and UA eGift Cards as different options, and the next screen opens a specific eGift Card product page.

requirements.text: The system shall support navigation from a gift-card discovery page to a detailed purchase page for a selected eGift card product.
The system shall allow a shopper to configure an eGift card purchase by entering recipient details, sender details, denomination, delivery date, and a personal message before purchase.
requirements.annotation_notes: Across the ordered screenshots, the eGift card form is progressively filled with recipient name, recipient email, sender email, amount, delivery date, and message.

requirements.text: The system shall support choosing an eGift card amount within a visible and fixed allowed denomination range.
requirements.annotation_notes: The product page displays a $10.00 to $500.00 range and later shows a $100 amount entry, but screenshots alone do not prove full range enforcement.

requirements.text: The system shall support scheduling eGift card delivery for a selected date rather than only immediate sending.
The system shall allow a shopper to include a personalized message with the eGift card.
requirements.annotation_notes: The message field is visible on the product page and later contains the entered text "gift card," directly showing support for personalized messaging.

requirements.text: The system shall provide an action for the configured eGift card on the product-detail page to get to the shopping cart.
requirements.annotation_notes: A prominent Add to Bag button remains available throughout the configuration flow, indicating that the product can be submitted to the shopping bag from this page.
requirements.manual_verification_notes: Add to bag -> shopping cart (less UI artifacts, naming part of UI design)

requirements.text: The system shall present key gift-card fulfillment and usage information, including delivery timing, redemption channels, fees or expiration status, and discount restrictions.
requirements.annotation_notes: The product page includes descriptive text and bullet points about email delivery timing, redemption locations, no fees or expiration dates, and ineligible discounts, though screenshots do not verify the backend truth of those claims.
requirements.manual_verification_notes: PF: the delivery date is set by the user and not a displayed timing by the website

requirements.text: The system shall allow customers to check the balance of an existing gift card by entering the card number and PIN.
requirements.annotation_notes: The gift-card landing page includes a Check Your Balance section with fields for card number and PIN and a View Balance action.

requirements.text: The system shall add the configured eGift card to the shopper's ongoing cart or bag session after confirmation so it can continue into downstream checkout.
requirements.annotation_notes: The visible flow ends with a completed purchase form and an Add to Bag action, which strongly implies downstream bag state, but the actual bag update is not shown.
requirements.manual_verification_notes:
added: "after confirmation"
system has to be instructed to do so

requirements.text: The system shall validate that required eGift card purchase information is present before accepting the item for purchase.
requirements.annotation_notes: The page is structured as a multi-field purchase form leading to Add to Bag, so required-field validation is a disciplined inference, even though no explicit error or success state is shown.
requirements.manual_verification_notes: the UI doesn't show different colors in the "Add to Bag" button, so it implies that the button wasn't turned of. We don't see a screen where the button was pressed beforehand but if an error occurs here that would also be fine.
